verbatim_text: Performance of the algorithm learned with the sampler and the reward. You compare the performance of that algorithm now with another algorithm that's trained on a curated data set using terminal reward. How do I define this? Essentially, it's data that you only know results in correct final answers. You could have gotten to that final answer in n number of ways. And it's curated using experts, using LLMs, whatnot. You only have access to this terminal reward function R in order to say this is a good trace. It's a correct trace. And that's how you curate it. Now, if you compare the performance of algorithms learned in either way, what we do find is that the performance gap between these two grows in the square root of the number of test time operations performed by the learned algorithm. So if you look at this performance plot and if you look at the x-axis as being the number of test time operations, now the algorithm that is trained on this curated data, if you compare that with the algorithm that's trained using our sampler and reward, the performance gap between these two grows in the square root of the x-axis, which is the number of test time operations. Roughly, that means that if you want to learn an algorithm that will perform well as you scale the number of test time operations, then you're pretty far off if you just learn curated data. Yes. Is performance between 0 and 1?
